chats.text: did anyone else get that email from HR about the compensation review window? i feel like im being personally targeted
{NAME}, you need to hear about the real reason behind my 'full-time offer.' it's not what they're saying. and neither is the 'fair shot' thing.
i just want to say — meeting over, but this conversation is not over for me. right?
{NAME}, i'm grateful for what you gave me. but also — it's not enough.
so i'm gonna go think about what i was told right?
did you tell anyone we were talking? this is weird man
if i stay will i get a raise at all like ever?
they said think it through but what if the thinking is just me overthinking
I tried to ask for a raise but got told not to bring it up. It's just... meetings about meetings? How do I feel aligned when this product isn't even my hobby?
Hey team, just looping in on the full-time offer discussion since we're all affected 👀
I think what resonates with me about that startup path is the possibility of making an impact and being myself, unlike here where everyone just talks in circles 💀
also my friends are building a startup and it feels real like i can make an impact there but here it's just... coffee and spreadsheets? am i wrong for thinking this?
okay team. going to take a day. will talk tomorrow.
lol just wanted to check if anyone else felt like they were running laps around a product nobody cares about 🏃‍♂️
what do we even talk about in meetings anymore? i feel like i'm just memorizing company values.
is it just me or does this place feel like a cult but with better healthcare? 🧘‍♂️
anyone else feel like their personality is slowly being erased by corporate speak? 'bandwidth' for 'time' 'synergy' for 'friendship' lol
I was made aware that my job description includes 'emotional labor' as a key deliverable.
You have 2 hours to consider your next move. Don't wait until the coffee machine breaks down for you again. I'll be monitoring my pulse.
I'm taking my laptop home and I expect a letterhead email from HR by EOD or we're going to have to do this whole 'process' again.
I was made aware that our 'collaborative discourse' is currently a series of 17-minute loops and I'm not looping anymore.
I want to be clear: my resignation is a statement. Not an exit. It's a statement that I have bandwidth for something better and this is not it.
{NAME}, if you're reading this, go find the coffee machine before they shut it down in the next meeting. The one where we talk about bandwidth.
I was made aware of everything and I was also made aware that 'making an impact' is currently defined by how many slides are on screen at once. My bandwidth is low.
I'm done looping. The offer is off the table and I was made aware that your bandwidth doesn't extend to post-departure sentiment analysis.
I'm not invested and that's my final word. Invested means you're all-in. I'm checking out. My bandwidth is yours no longer.
